title: Time & Clock Bilingual Classroom Labels
description: A set of bilingual (English-Spanish) labels, routine cards, and rules posters designed for K-2 classrooms to introduce and reinforce time-related vocabulary and daily routines.

visual_routine_cards.icon_prompt: children sitting in a circle, teacher, book
child reading a book quietly, shhh gesture
child leaving classroom, backpack, clock, school bus

classroom_rules.icon_prompt: child listening, ear icon, teacher speaking
child with raised hand, question mark
child putting away toys, broom, tidy up

teacher_notes.objective: To introduce and reinforce basic time-related vocabulary and daily classroom routines in both English and Spanish, supporting language acquisition and classroom management for K-2 ELL newcomers.
teacher_notes.suggested_use: Place full-size labels near the classroom clock, calendar, or a dedicated 'Time' learning center. Use small labels on objects or areas to indicate 'now' (e.g., current activity bin) or 'later' (e.g., future activity bin). Display visual routine cards in a prominent location, reviewing them daily. Post classroom rules where they are easily visible and refer to them frequently.
materials_needed: Velcro dots (optional, for interactive routine charts)
A classroom clock with clear numbers (digital and analog if possible)
differentiation_tips: Pair visuals with gestures and actions for each word/routine.
Encourage students to repeat the words in both languages.
Use sentence frames (e.g., 'It is [time].', 'First we do [routine], then we do [routine].')
Integrate the labels into daily conversations and calendar time.
For rules, role-play scenarios to demonstrate expected behaviors.
